source_alignment.subject: Science
source_alignment.source_statement: Energy changes and transfers, including fuels and energy resources, heating and thermal equilibrium, and comparing energy values.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This Year 7 pack introduces energy as a stored-and-transferred quantity through safe, explorable transfer models rather than as something objects use up or contain as a visible fluid.

objective.child_goal: I can describe how energy is stored and transferred in simple systems.
objective.statement: Identify stores of energy, describe pathways of energy transfer, and explain useful and dissipated transfers in everyday systems.
misconceptions: Thinks energy is a substance that is used up or disappears.
Confuses energy stores with transfer pathways such as heating, radiation or electrical work.
Assumes a transfer is only important when it is useful and ignores dissipated energy.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A way energy is stored, such as in a hot object, a battery or a moving object.
vocabulary.audio_script: An energy store is a way energy is stored in a system.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A labelled store icon inside a system boundary.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A process that moves energy from one store to another.
vocabulary.audio_script: A transfer moves energy between stores.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: An arrow connecting two store icons.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Spread out to the surroundings so it is less useful.
vocabulary.audio_script: Dissipated energy is spread out to the surroundings.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Small gentle arrows spreading into the room.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Choose what changes when a torch is switched on.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Reactivate everyday examples while separating observable effects from energy reasoning.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: We can observe light and warmth, then use an energy model to explain the transfers.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Torch, battery and surroundings appear as simple system cards.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Build an energy story using stores and transfer arrows.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce stores and pathways as a model with precise roles.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Stores say where energy is held. Pathways say how energy is transferred.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A battery store card connects to a lamp store and surroundings by arrow pathways.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Follow the energy from the battery to the lamp and surroundings.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model useful and dissipated transfers without implying energy disappears.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Energy is transferred electrically from the chemical store in the battery. Some is transferred by radiation as light and some by heating to the surroundings.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Colour-coded arrows move from battery chemical store to lamp and surroundings.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Adjust the insulation slider. What happens to the transfer to the surroundings?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Connect model variables to rates and pathways of heating.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Changing insulation changes how quickly energy is transferred by heating to the surroundings.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Thermal store bar and surroundings arrows respond to the slider.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Is heating a store or a transfer pathway?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Detect confusion between stores and pathways.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Heating is a transfer pathway. Thermal energy store is the store.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Heating arrow is sorted away from thermal store card.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Ori why energy is not destroyed when a phone gets warm.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Repair the used-up-energy misconception through conservation and dissipation language.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: I thought the energy vanished. Can you show where it was transferred?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Companion points to a warm phone and surrounding air arrows.

manipulatives.purpose: Let learners assemble store cards, pathway arrows and system boundaries for torches, kettles, phones, bouncing balls and insulated cups.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners choose stores, drag or switch-select pathway arrows, adjust one variable, freeze the model and write a useful-plus-dissipated energy story.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Includes reduced-motion arrow pulses, static transfer snapshots, high-contrast labels, keyboard and switch scanning, audio descriptions, and a low-sensory mode with calm colours and no rapid particle-like effects.

practice.interleaving_notes: Interleave stores, pathways and dissipation with particles and forces contexts; use short retrieval items before quantitative efficiency work.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Separate energy stores from transfer pathways in varied everyday systems.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: system image + mixed store/pathway cards + diagnostic feedback
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use KS3 language: thermal store, chemical store, kinetic store; heating and radiation as pathways.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Construct complete energy stories with useful and dissipated transfers.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: system boundary + starting store + target effect + draggable pathway arrows + explanation stem
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Do not show energy as a fluid leaking away; arrows represent transfers, not stuff.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Explain why energy transferred to surroundings is conserved but less useful.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: scenario + useful effect + warm/sound surroundings clue + four explanation choices
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Avoid moral language such as wasted unless paired with scientific dissipated wording.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Use prediction-observation-explanation with insulation, surface colour or power changes.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: predict transfer change + observe simulator readout + explain using store/pathway language
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep variables safe and classroom-plausible; no hazardous heating instructions.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Maintain energy vocabulary across later forces, electricity and particle lessons.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: short mixed retrieval prompt + one misconception distractor
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Prefer concise prompts suitable for daily warm-up orbit.

body.prompt: Build the best energy story for a torch that is switched on.

hints: Start with the store in the battery.
Include what is useful and what is transferred to the surroundings.
question_variants.explanation: Energy is transferred electrically from the chemical store in the battery. Some energy is transferred by radiation as light and some by heating to the surroundings.

body.prompt: Sort each card as an energy store or a transfer pathway.

value: store: thermal store, chemical store, kinetic store

hints: A store says where energy is held.
A pathway says how energy moves between stores.
question_variants.explanation: Thermal, chemical and kinetic are stores. Heating and radiation are transfer pathways.

body.prompt: A phone gets warm while charging. Which explanation is most scientific?
choices: Some energy is transferred by heating to the phone and surroundings; it is dissipated, not destroyed.
Energy has disappeared because the phone used it up.
Heating is the energy store inside the charger.
Only useful light transfers count as energy transfers.

expected_answer.value: Some energy is transferred by heating to the phone and surroundings; it is dissipated, not destroyed.

hints: Warm surroundings are evidence of a transfer.
question_variants.explanation: Energy transferred to the phone and surroundings by heating is conserved but becomes spread out and less useful.

body.prompt: Predict what happens when the cup is given thicker insulation, observe the simulator, then choose the best explanation.
prediction_options: Energy transfers by heating to the surroundings more slowly
body.observation: The temperature bar falls more slowly and the surroundings arrows become smaller.

expected_answer.value: Energy transfers by heating to the surroundings more slowly

hints: Watch the transfer arrows to the surroundings.
Insulation changes the rate of transfer, not the meaning of the stores.
question_variants.explanation: Thicker insulation reduces the rate of energy transfer by heating to the surroundings, so the cup's thermal store decreases more slowly.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Thinks energy is a substance that is used up or disappears.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner says the energy has gone or vanished after a device becomes warm.
repair_sequence: Show the starting store and all output arrows in a closed energy story.
Freeze the model and label useful transfer and dissipated transfer separately.
Ask the learner to complete the sentence: energy was transferred to...
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner states that energy is transferred and dissipated rather than destroyed.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Confuses energy stores with transfer pathways such as heating, radiation or electrical work.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner places heating, radiation or electrical transfer in the store category.
repair_sequence: Sort cards using the test question: where is it held, or how does it move?
Animate a pathway arrow between two store cards.
Ask learner to explain one store and one pathway for the same system.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner correctly classifies thermal store as a store and heating as a pathway.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Assumes a transfer is only important when it is useful and ignores dissipated energy.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner builds only the useful output arrow and omits surroundings.
repair_sequence: Add temperature or sound evidence from the surroundings.
Compare useful and dissipated arrows with different labels.
Ask learner to revise the energy story to include surroundings.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner includes at least one dissipated transfer in the model.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use calm dark-lab colours, no flashing energy effects and a single highlighted pathway at a time.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace moving arrows with numbered static snapshots and optional slow pulse animation.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Narrate each store and transfer from left to right with replayable vocabulary cards.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use icon-supported store cards, short explanation stems and a persistent glossary for store, transfer and dissipated.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Limit the active card bank to the current system and reveal dissipated transfers only after the useful pathway is placed.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow freeze-frame inspection, step-by-step replay and side-by-side before/after simulator panels.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Frame wrong energy stories as models to improve using evidence from the simulator.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach store, transfer, pathway, surroundings, useful, dissipated, heating, radiation and electrical with paired icons.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires identifying stores and transfer pathways in simple systems; secure requires explaining useful and dissipated transfers using conservation language.
evidence.parent_summary: Can describe how energy is stored and transferred in everyday systems, including energy spread to surroundings.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Builds store-pathway models, distinguishes stores from pathways and explains dissipation without saying energy is destroyed.
next_actions: Connect energy transfers to heating and particles after transfer language is secure.
Introduce simple energy resource comparisons and efficiency once useful/dissipated reasoning is stable.
Interleave with forces where work done transfers energy.

qa.notes: Review-status Year 7 energy sample with SEND-safe simulator interactions, reduced-motion transfer models and explicit conservation/dissipation misconception repair.
